nativeRange.habitat: Tropical Southeast Asian aroid, generally reported growing along the banks and beds of fast-flowing rainforest streams and on damp forest floors in Borneo, Sumatra and surrounding regions; many populations are emersed or periodically submerged

decorPreferences: lava rock, driftwood or other porous hardscape for the rhizome to attach to
moderate flow across the rhizome to mimic stream conditions
shaded or moderately lit areas; suits midground or foreground placement
introduce only into stable, established tanks — sensitive to swings during acclimation

goodWith: shrimp (Neocaridina and Caridina) which graze biofilm without harming the plant
other low-light epiphytes such as Anubias, Bucephalandra, Java fern and mosses
avoidWith: large herbivorous or rhizome-disturbing fish such as goldfish, silver dollars and large plecos
vigorous diggers (large cichlids) that may dislodge attached rhizomes
newly set up tanks with unstable parameters

summary: Homalomena is a slow-growing rhizome aroid traded in the aquarium hobby much like Anubias and Bucephalandra. Many species and trade forms are sold; the rhizome should be attached to hardscape rather than buried, and most plants arrive emersed-grown and need time to transition to fully submerged growth.
careNotes: Homalomena is a tropical Southeast Asian genus of roughly 160 aroids in family Araceae (order Alismatales). In the aquarium trade it is treated as a 'specialty rhizome aroid' alongside Anubias and Bucephalandra: a creeping rhizome with waxy, often coral-pink to deep-green leaves that should be attached to lava rock, driftwood or other porous hardscape with cyanoacrylate gel, thread or fishing line and left exposed to the water column. Burying the rhizome generally causes it to rot. Buce Plant's product pages for Homalomena humilis and Homalomena insignis both report water parameters of pH ~6.0-7.5 with low-to-medium lighting, CO2 'not necessary but recommended', moderate growth rate, and an adult size of roughly 4-10 in (10-25 cm) depending on species. Temperature is rarely stated explicitly by retailers for this genus; standard tropical-aquarium 22-28 °C is appropriate by analogy to Bucephalandra and the genus' native rainforest streams. Most Homalomena imported into the trade are commercially grown emersed (Sumatra, Borneo, Papua), and the submerged transition can be tricky — many specimens shed their original leaves and grow new submersed-form leaves over weeks, similar to 'buce melt'. As long as the rhizome stays firm, recovery is typically reported. Many trade names exist (H. humilis, H. insignis, H. rubescens, H. sekadau, H. 'King of Hearts', H. 'Sub-Hasei', H. Silver, H. Samurai, H. Jambi); this genus-level entry covers shared husbandry. Several species are also kept as terrarium/paludarium or houseplant subjects and may not adapt to fully submerged life. Note on sourcing: the Stage A discovery pass identified Tropica as the intended primary source, but Tropica does not currently catalogue any Homalomena species; Buce Plant's two distinct species pages (humilis and insignis) were used as the primary and secondary sources, with Wikipedia used for taxonomic confirmation. Reviewer should sanity-check the genus-level water parameters once a species-specific trade source becomes available.
breedingNotes: Hobby propagation is by rhizome division: once established with new leaves and roots, cut the rhizome at a natural bend with clean sharp scissors leaving each piece with several leaves and visible roots, and reattach to a separate piece of hardscape (or plant into nutrient-rich substrate for species that root). Sexual reproduction via the Araceae spathe-and-spadix inflorescence is occasionally seen in emersed and paludarium setups but is not a practical aquarium propagation method.

primary.name: Buce Plant — Homalomena humilis
primary.notes: Used as the effective primary source after the controller-supplied Tropica URL was confirmed not to catalogue any Homalomena species (Tropica's plant catalogue jumps from Hygrophila to other genera). Buce Plant's humilis page provides the family (Araceae), Borneo native range, pH 6-7.5, adult size 4-10 in (10-25 cm), low-to-medium lighting, CO2 optional, moderate growth, foreground/midground or hardscape placement, and rhizome-cutting propagation.

additional.name: Buce Plant — Homalomena insignis
additional.notes: Cross-checked genus husbandry on a second species page. Confirmed Araceae, Borneo origin, pH 6-7.5, adult size 4-10 in (10-25 cm), low-to-medium lighting, CO2 optional-but-recommended, moderate growth, mid- or foreground placement (hardscape or substrate), nutrient-rich substrate preference, propagation by rhizome cutting. Agreement with the humilis page on every parameter that both report.

additional.name: Buce Plant — Homalomena Sub-Hasei
additional.notes: Used for native-range diversity (Sumatra in addition to Borneo) and to confirm that some Homalomena trade forms are essentially terrestrial/terrarium subjects (described as 'compact evergreen perennial' for low-light terrariums/vivariums/paludariums).

additional.name: Wikipedia — Homalomena
additional.notes: Taxonomy fallback: confirmed family Araceae, order Alismatales, ~160 species in tropical Asia (Borneo, Sumatra, Philippines, Taiwan) and the neotropics (some reclassified as Adelonema). Used only for taxonomy, not husbandry.
